item.title: How the Iran war could hurt Wall Street
item.description: The big unknown is how the conflict could alter the Gulf’s investment priorities in the next year or two

item.title: The return of the e-merging markets
item.description: For now South Korea and Taiwan are the biggest beneficiaries of the AI wave

item.title: Out of office, still hyper-responsive
item.description: Once established by professional training, the addictive pull of screens, newsfeeds and email replies is impossible to shake

item.title: Landlords need to wise up about the Renters’ Rights Act
item.description: Next week, the biggest overhaul of England’s private rented sector in decades comes into effect — yet many have still not engaged with the changes

item.title: Risk management must evolve to remain fit for purpose
item.description: Resilience, crisis preparedness and contingency planning must take centre stage

item.title: China does have one powerful lever to boost consumption
item.description: A permanent cut in the payroll tax could benefit workers and the world

item.title: The US and Europe are still stronger together
item.description: Despite Trump and the war with Iran, Brussels’ interests remain deeply entwined with Washington’s

item.title: The stock market’s new approach to valuation
item.description: It is demonstrating an Earnings Before Iran, Tariffs and Dubious Announcements (ebitda) mentality

item.title: Lessons from the China shock 2.0
item.description: The response should be better industrial strategies, not knee-jerk protectionism

item.title: Of course Trump thinks he’s the Messiah — his disciples tell him so
item.description: The ‘devout’ Christians in the US president’s life are really just fawning sycophants

item.title: The NHS’s deal with Palantir could be good for its financial health
item.description: Medical data sits in different software systems — connecting the dots should save time, beds and money

item.title: The annoyance economy isn’t going anywhere
item.description: We must find our own amusements in dealing with spam, robocalls and chatbots

item.title: The weird world of work perks
item.description: Companies are reining in benefits, but workers are unlikely to miss some of them as much as you might think

item.title: Chart of the Week: Are Treasuries losing their haven status?
item.description: Bonds have not been a good hedge during the war

item.title: Why higher pay hasn’t made young adults feel richer
item.description: The aspiration gap has turned everyone into losers, especially graduates

item.title: London’s riverside properties tell conflicting stories about UK market
item.description: Struggles to sell even in popular areas are a timely reminder of the challenges created by high interest rates

item.title: The return of Londonophobia
item.description: Reflections on an ancient prejudice

item.title: Defence dithering is harming the UK
item.description: As the transatlantic alliance falters, Starmer needs to step up

item.title: The declining appeal of the Hong Kong expat
item.description: Demand for mainlanders with Mandarin skills is on the rise

item.title: Jancis Robinson’s A-Z of 21st century wine — part two
item.description: A guide to orange wine, microbial terroir, regenerative viticulture and more

item.title: Sitting comfortably? The portrait is making a comeback
item.description: In the age of AI, paintings feel real again

item.title: Launderettes may be coming back into financial fashion
item.description: A shift towards more laundry being done out of the home is creating new opportunities

item.title: Cometa, Fitzrovia: Mexican delights, and some of London’s best fried chicken — restaurant review
item.description: Chef residency restaurant Carousel has made a smart choice for its first permanent space

item.title: When keeping it in cash is the dangerous option
item.description: Having a safe store of ready money is wise. But holding a significant proportion of assets in cash is a big long-term risk to savings

item.title: Estate agent listings are a great subject for conceptual art
item.description: Every element of the housing experience is being reimagined as art — and in a property listing the intangible meaning or possibility is ripe for creative exploitation
